items.title: Yardeni Says Inflation, Fed Back in Play as Iran Crisis Returns
items.description: Market strategist Ed Yardeni said the rupture in the ceasefire between the US and Iran risks sparking a fresh acceleration in price growth, which in turn could compel the Federal Reserve to raise inte

items.title: UK Defense Chiefs Push Burnham to Consider Issuing War Bonds
items.description: Senior defense officials are pushing Andy Burnham to reconsider loosening the UK’s fiscal rules to help fund its military, including by issuing war bonds.

items.title: Omani Shipping Firm Asyad’s Owner Is Said to Weigh Stake Sale
items.description: A conglomerate backed by Oman’s sovereign wealth fund is looking to sell a stake worth in the shipping business it listed last year, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: Stocks Drop, Oil Jumps After Trump Says Ceasefire with Iran Is "Over" | Bloomberg Brief 07/08/2026
items.description: US equity futures slump and oil extends gains as President Trump says a tentative ceasefire with Iran is over. The US had struck 80 sites in Iran ahead of the president's remarks at the NATO Summit in

items.title: Romania Keeps EU’s Highest Rates With Inflation Stuck Above 10%
items.description: Romania held interest rates at the highest level in the European Union as policymakers tackle double digit inflation in an economy on the verge of a recession.

items.title: Susquehanna Puts Up $500 Million for ‘Hedging’ World Cup Games
items.description: Susquehanna International Group has lined up $500 million to work with institutions that want to use prediction markets to hedge the economic risks associated with various World Cup outcomes.

items.title: AI Firm Zhipu to Sell $4 Billion of Shares After 1,500% Rally
items.description: Chinese artificial-intelligence model maker Zhipu is seeking to raise about $4 billion from a share sale after its stock soared almost 1,500% since a January listing in Hong Kong.

items.title: Pricey Credit Markets Fuel Funds That Can Buy Anything
items.description: Bond investors are increasingly betting on flexible funds that can buy whatever they like as lofty credit market valuations leave little margin for error.

items.title: Indian Stocks, Rupee Slide After Trump Reignites Iran Tensions
items.description: Indian stocks tumbled and the rupee weakened after oil prices gained following US President Donald Trump’s remark that the tentative ceasefire with Iran had ended after recent attacks on ships transit

items.title: Coffee Prices to Stay High as Volatility Lingers, Lavazza Says
items.description: The latest bout of volatility in the coffee market is set to linger, complicating the outlook for consumers hoping to see lower prices, according to Italian roaster Luigi Lavazza SpA.

items.title: Argentina to Repay $4 Billion, Defying Critics Who Doubted Tack
items.description: Argentina is set to make a major payment on its dollar bonds this week, pulling off a feat few investors thought possible while the country refuses to tap global debt markets.

items.title: Trump & Iran Back in Focus, Korean Stocks Tumble | The Opening Trade 7/8/2026
items.description:
Stocks fell, bond yields spiked and oil rallied after President Donald Trump thrust geopolitical risks back into focus by declaring the ceasefire between the US and Iran to be over.

items.title: US Heat Sends Power Demand Far Beyond Forecasts
items.description: The country’s biggest grid operator says consumption likely hit a record, smashing a high that had stood for about 20 years.

items.title: State Bank of India Draws Over $1.5 Billion in Offshore Deposits
items.description: State Bank of India has so far raised more than $1.5 billion from foreign-currency deposits under a special program launched last month for overseas citizens, people familiar with the matter said.

items.title: Allianz Unit to Cut as Many as 1,800 Jobs in Push to Adopt AI
items.description: Allianz SE plans to eliminate hundreds of positions at its assistance and travel insurance subsidiary Allianz Partners as the company pushes forward with implementing artificial intelligence solutions

items.title: Wave of Earnings Surprises Will Be Hard to Repeat, Says Goldman
items.description: The wave of AI-fueled earnings surprises that powered stocks in the last earning season will be tough to repeat, making it unlikely the results alone will spark a major rally, according to Christian M

items.title: India Seeks Safe Hormuz Passage for Nine Ships as Truce Falters

items.title: Nvidia Is World’s Most Valuable Company and Cheaper Than Hershey
items.description: Nvidia is relatively cheap despite the continued domination of its chips in AI data centers.

items.title: Dream Finders Homes Boosts Bid for Beazer to $875 Million
items.description: Dream Finders Homes Inc. boosted its takeover offer for rival homebuilder Beazer Homes USA Inc., which said it’s received interest from other potential suitors.
